title: EventualResponse
description: Event: `eventual_response`. The terminal event of a streaming turn. Emitted after the agent workflow completes and its output has been persisted. Clients should treat this as the authoritative final state for the turn and may discard intermediate `stream_chunk` / `stream_token` data. Status is always 200 on success.

requestId.description: Echoes the `requestId` from the originating `send_message` action.

status.description: HTTP-like status. Always 200 for a successful eventual response.

requestId.description: The request ID this response belongs to.

messageId.description: ID of the agent message as persisted to the `conversation_messages` table.

response.description: The structured agent response payload. Shape depends on the agent template. Clients should validate against a template-specific schema before rendering.

needsEscalation.description: True if the agent flagged this conversation for human escalation.

escalationReason.description: Human-readable escalation reason when `needsEscalation` is true.

citations.description: The sources that grounded this answer, when any were retrieved. Collected by the runtime from the documents that actually grounded the turn — the auto-injected `[Relevant knowledge]` context and any `knowledge_search` tool results — deduplicated by source id and capped. Optional and back-compatible: absent when the turn used no knowledge sources. Each item is a `Citation` (see `domain/citation.schema.json`).

items.description: A source the agent used to ground its answer (a `Citation` — see `domain/citation.schema.json`). For GitHub-sourced documents `url` is the blob/issue URL; documents without a web source omit it.

id.description: Stable identifier of the cited source document (the knowledge-base `document_id`). Used to deduplicate citations within a turn.

title.description: Human-readable label for the source — typically the document's source path or, for web-sourced docs, the URL/title.

url.description: Canonical link to the source, when one exists. For GitHub-sourced documents this is the blob/issue URL stamped onto the document's `source` at ingest. Absent for sources with no web location.

snippet.description: The retrieved chunk text that grounded the answer, truncated to a bounded length for display.

score.description: Relevance score of this source for the turn's query (the knowledge-base similarity score). Higher is more relevant.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the event was emitted.
